pattern: Because the array is already sorted, the absolute difference to every earlier element is just the current value times its position minus their prefix sum, and symmetrically for every later element, so a single prefix-sum pass replaces a quadratic all-pairs comparison.
hints: Since the array is sorted, for a fixed index every element before it is smaller (or equal) and every element after it is larger (or equal), so the absolute value bars can be dropped in favor of a direct subtraction.

The total distance to all earlier elements at index i is (value * i) minus the sum of those i elements; the total distance to all later elements is the sum of those elements minus (value * their count).
Track a running prefix sum while scanning left to right, and keep the total array sum computed once up front, so each index's answer is O(1) work.

edge_inputs.description: a small sorted array with distinct values
a sorted array with a repeated value in the middle
all elements identical, every distance is zero
values span the full allowed range, testing for overflow-prone accumulation
a long run of equal values followed by one distinct value

stress.description: large sorted array with many repeats
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 100000))
    nums = sorted(rng.randint(1, 10000) for _ in range(n))
    return [nums]

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def getSumAbsoluteDifferences(self, nums):
        n = len(nums)
        total = sum(nums)
        prefix = 0
        res = []
        for i, x in enumerate(nums):
            left = x * i - prefix
            right = (total - prefix - x) - x * (n - i - 1)
            res.append(left + right)
            prefix += x
        return res
